headline: War in Iran: Europe's complicit silence on international law
description: European reluctance to condemn the Israeli-American intervention in Iran flies in the face of the continent's values. No one can believe that Benjamin Netanyahu and Donald Trump's goal is to defend the interests of the Iranian people.

headline: Trump says war on Iran will end 'very soon,' calls conflict 'very complete'
description: The comments helped lift markets and ease energy fears even as details on any ceasefire remain vague.

headline: Israel's double-edged ambitions in Lebanon
description: The Israeli army is seeking to complete the destructive work it began during the autumn 2024 war and to bring Hezbollah to its knees. Yet its airstrikes, which have caused numerous civilian casualties, could paradoxically restore legitimacy to the Shiite militia and plunge Lebanon into lasting chaos.

headline: 'The new Iran will have a crucial asset: Its diaspora'
description: Researchers Mohammad-Reza Djalili and Thierry Kellner argue that, despite deep ideological divisions, Iranians living abroad are poised to play a decisive role in shaping the regime that could emerge in the aftermath of the current conflict.

headline: One week before France's local elections, electorate leans right, poll shows
description: With environmental issues taking a back seat, security issues coming to the fore and a clear increase in support for the far-right Rassemblement National, the survey conducted by Ipsos BVA for Le Monde, Cevipof and the Jean Jaurès Foundation paints an unprecedented picture of the French political landscape.

Mojtaba Khamenei, a new supreme leader consolidating the power of Iran's conservative factions
description: The appointment of Ali Khamenei's son, a little-known figure with close ties to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, signals the Iranian regime's intention to maintain the political status quo, as war with Israel and the US continues.

headline: Cultural heritage is under threat from the Middle East war
description: Several sites, including Tehran's Golestan Palace and Tel Aviv's Bauhaus buildings, have sustained damaged from missile strikes.

headline: Tracey Emin has art in her blood: 'Each of her works is both testimony and proof that she survived and has been able to give form to trauma'
description: A rebellious and accomplished figure on the British art scene, Emin, who has been affected by illness, has returned to live in Margate, the seaside resort where she grew up. The artist explains how she is developing a microcosm of galleries, schools and studios there, aimed at fostering a new generation of creators. She also looks back on her chaotic career, as the Tate Modern dedicates a retrospective to her.
